rulings.Case Title: FUNG LEE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, Line 5, DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, and ANNE M. SUSKIND, M.D.'s Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Defendants. Defendant's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production per CCP 437(p)(2). The court sustains plaintiff's objections to exhibit 1 to the Stayduhar declaration (purported medical records) on the grounds of hearsay and lack of authentication. "Although hospital and medical records are hearsay, they can be admitted under the business records exception to the hearsay rule. [citations] Such records, however, must be properly authenticated." (Garibay v. Hemmat (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 735, 742.) "Without those hospital records, and without testimony providing for authentication of such records, [the expert] declaration had no evidentiary basis." (Id.) Here, the alleged medical records are attached to the declaration of counsel Stayduhar. She merely states: "I certify that the documents attached hereto as Exhibit 1 are true and correct copies of medical records pertaining to decedent Gim Lee from UCSF medical Center." (Stayduhar Decl., par. 2.) Counsel fails to demonstrate any personal knowledge for this assertion and does not authenticate the proffered medical records. Also attached to this declaration is a custodian of record declaration. That declaration does not fill the evidentiary gulf. The declaration does not comply with Evidence Code section 1561(a)(3) and (5) because it does not set forth "[a] description of the mode of preparation of the records" nor that "[t]he records were prepared by the personnel of the business in the ordinary course of business." There is insufficient evidence to qualify the attachments as business records excepted from the hearsay rule. (Evidence Code sec. 1271.) The court further notes that the custodian declaration does not meet the subscription requirement of CCP 2015.5. The declaration was not made "under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California" nor is it clear where the declaration was signed. Given all these evidentiary failings, the motion is denied in its entirety. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH B. PAULO VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, Line 6, DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. Plaintiff's request to stay this case pending the California Supreme Court's resolution of Rodriguez v. FCA US, LLC (2022) 77 Cal.App.5th 209 is granted. (CCP 128(a)(8).) The main issue in this case is whether the express warranty provisions of the Song-Beverly Act apply to used vehicles. There is a split of authority regarding that issue and the California Supreme Court recently heard argument on the matter. This court anticipates that the split between Rodriguez and Stiles v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2024) 101 Cal.App.5th 913, which is also under review, will be resolved shortly. Defendant may seek to lift the stay and calendar an updated summary judgment motion after the California Supreme Court issues its decision. rulings.Case Title: QURAN BLAKEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (ERRONEOUSLY SUED, SEPARATELY, AS SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO)'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to Quran Blakey's first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. Blakey alleges she was exiting an airplane when she was assaulted by John Doe, and employee of defendants United Airlines and the City. Blakey sued both entities for negligence and negligent hiring claims. The court overruled the City's demurrer as to the negligence claim and gave Blakey leave to amend her negligent hiring claim. She did so, and the City once again demurs to the fifth cause of action for negligent hiring. First, the City contends that John Doe cannot plausibly be an employee of both the airline and the City. Discovery will clarify the facts, and meanwhile the City has sufficient notice of an alleged incident involving its employee. Second, the City argues Blakey fails to plead a special relationship between Blakey and the City that gives rise to the duty to protect her from the alleged negligently hired John Doe. (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861 [school district and employees have a special relationship with their students giving rise to a viable negligent hiring claim].) Blakey's amended complaint now pleads at least two potential special relationship theories: that of a business entity and its patrons, and common carrier and its passengers (FAC 36, 37). These allegations suffice at the pleadings stage to support her negligent hiring claim. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: ISAIAH VELARDE ET AL VS. VOLVO CARS OF NORTH AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, Line 8, 1- DEFENDANT VOLVO CAR USA LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS VOLVO CARS OF NORTH AMERICA, INC.)'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Volvo Car USA, LLC's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled with one exception noted below. The FAC pleads that Isaiah Velarde stopped his Volvo XC90 to turn and another vehicle traveling the same direction impacted it. (Id. at 3:20-4:6.) The Volvo's steering wheel turned involuntarily, snapping Velarde's right arm to expose bone. (Id. at 3:20-4:6, 11:15-18.) Volvo first demurs that to allege products liability the FAC had to specify what the defective parts were. (Dmr. 2:13-16.) But Volvo cites no authority for this notion and it is contrary to California's liberal pleading standards. Volvo also demurs that to allege breach of warranty the FAC had to specify when Velarde's Volvo was purchased and whether it was new. (Dmr. 3:1-2.) Again, Volvo cites no authority for this notion and it is contrary to California's liberal pleading standards. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to plead warranty terms, as to which demurrer is sustained. (Dmr. 3:4-5.) Punitive damages are addressed on the accompanying motion to strike. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: BOUTIQUE AIR, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPOATION VS. AIN MEDIA GROUP, INC. A COLORADO CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2024, Line 9, 3- DEFENDANT AIN MEDIA GROUP, INC. A COLORADO CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant AIN Media Group, Inc.'s special motion to strike Boutique Air, Inc's second amended complaint is granted. On prong one of the anti-SLAPP analysis, AIN meets its burden of showing the article is protected under the catchall provision 425.16(e)(4). The article contributes to the public debate on consumer safety, specifically airline safety, by identifying issues with Boutique's performance and safety record. (Mem. p. 9.) On prong two, Boutique fails to show a probability of prevailing on its libel claims. These claims are based partly on statements concerning an official FAA proceeding that are privileged under Civil Code 47(d), and partly on facts describing past safety incidents that Boutique has not shown are false. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: BOUTIQUE AIR, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPOATION VS. AIN MEDIA GROUP, INC. A COLORADO CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Obtain Limited Discovery Pursuant To Section 425.16(G) Of Code Of Civil Procedure
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2024, Line 9, 4- PLAINTIFF BOUTIQUE AIR, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPOATION's Motion for Leave to Obtain Limited Discovery Pursuant To Section 425.16(G) Of Code Of Civil Procedure. Plaintiff Boutique Air, Inc.'s motion for limited discovery is denied. Discovery is generally stayed upon filing an anti-SLAPP motion, but CCP 425.16(g) allows the court to permit discovery upon a showing of good cause. (Balla v. Hall (2021) 59 Cal.App.5th 654, 692.) Good cause requires a showing that the specified discovery is necessary for the plaintiff to oppose the anti-SLAPP motion and is tailored to that end. (Id.) "Obviously, the purpose of the statute would be frustrated if the plaintiff could drag on proceedings for many months by claiming a need to conduct additional investigation." (Ludwig v. Superior Ct. (1995) 37 Cal. App. 4th 8, 16.) Here, Boutique argues it needs discovery to show the article does not fall under the anti-SLAPP statute's catchall provision, CCP 425.16(e)(4). Boutique argues that discovery on AIN's readership will show that contextually, the speech does not contribute to the public debate. As discussed in the companion order granting Colleen Mondor's special motion to strike, the article falls under this catchall provision regardless of the magazine's readership. Thus, Boutique has not established good cause for discovery on AIN's readership. Next, Boutique argues it requires discovery on whether the statements were published with actual malice. The court did not base its decision on the actual malice, so no discovery on this issue is needed. Finally, Boutique argues it needs discovery on the truth or falsity of the allegedly defamatory statements. Many of these statements are absolutely privileged under Civil Code 47(d). The remaining statements concern Boutique's self-reported safety incidents and business woes. Boutique would have the best access to this information, so discovery is unnecessary. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: RUBEN MARTINEZ VS. SFHD PARTNERS, LLC DBA SAN FRANCISCO HARLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2024, Line 10, PLAINTIFF RUBEN MARTINEZ's Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches a copy of the report and recommendation and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS ROSARIO LAM VS. BRIGHT FITNESS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2024, Line 12, DEFENDANT NERISSA ZHANG'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Defendant Nerissa Zhang's "motion to change venue" is denied. A court in a county where any defendant resides when the action began is a "proper court for trial of the action" (CCP 395(a)). And defendant Bright Fitness Inc. stated in an official document signed by Zhang that its "principal executive office" was in San Francisco. (RJN Ex A.) Zhang's bare bones motion also refers to "convenience of the witnesses" and "existence of a related case," but fails to carry her burdens to demonstrate either. (Mot. 1:23-26; Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Sup. Ct. (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 744 [movant's "burden of negativing plaintiff's choice of venue" with "competent evidence"].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ALEXANDER ET AL VS. CHARLES VEILLEUX ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2024, Line 14, DEFENDANTS CHARLES VEILLEUX, CHARLES VEILLEUX & ASSOCIES, AND STEPHEN CLARKE'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. This action regards plaintiff California lawyers funding a toxic tort class action by defendant lawyers in Canada. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is overruled. Exhibits A, B, C and D are part of the complaint and indicate that all plaintiffs and defendants are proper parties; discovery can be propounded. This is the pleading stage and the complaint adequately alleges claims for accounting, breach of contract and restitution under California's liberal rules. (See Thomas v. Regents of University of California (2023) 97 Cal.App.5th 587, 605.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
